---
title: "Preview a recipe DAG"
subtitle: "Turn a plan into a recipe and inspect its graph before execution."
sidebar: cookbook
---

## Use when

You want to review how extraction steps depend on inputs and on one another
before running them.

```{python}
from natural_features.core.recipe import as_mermaid
from natural_features.workflows import plan_features

plan = plan_features(
    "The scene opens on a quiet room.",
    features=["text.tokenize"],
)
recipe = plan.to_recipe()
recipe
```

## Render the graph

```{python}
print(as_mermaid(recipe))
```

## What the graph lets you inspect

The arrows show which steps read the original inputs and which steps read an
earlier step's output. Use the graph to spot a wrong input or a missing
dependency before execution. Creating the graph does not run any extractor.

## See also

[Recipes and execution graphs](../concepts/recipes-and-dags.qmd)
